rootCauseClusters.name: Unguarded data[0] Access Pattern
rootCauseClusters.description: After .insert().select() or .update().select(), code accesses data[0] without checking array length. Empty arrays from RLS or row deletion produce undefined, breaking JSON serialization.

rootCauseClusters.name: Incomplete PostgREST Sanitization
rootCauseClusters.description: sanitizeFilterValue strips commas and parentheses but not periods, which are structural in PostgREST field.op.value syntax. User input with periods corrupts .or() filters.

rootCauseClusters.name: Error Type Conflation
rootCauseClusters.description: Some handlers collapse all error types into a single response path. DB connection failures appear as 'not found' to users, and single-query failures discard results from successful parallel queries.

findings.description: create_customer accesses data[0] without checking if data is a non-empty array. If Supabase returns empty array (e.g. RLS blocks read-back), jsonResponse(undefined) produces malformed MCP response.
findings.suggestion: Add guard: if (!data || data.length === 0) return textResponse('Customer created but could not be retrieved'); before accessing data[0].

findings.description: create_ticket accesses data[0] without null check. Same RLS/empty-array vulnerability as create_customer.
findings.suggestion: Add same guard as create_customer before data[0] access.

findings.description: close_ticket accesses data[0] after update without checking array length. Row deleted between read and write (e.g. customer cascade delete) produces undefined.
findings.suggestion: Add guard: if (!data || data.length === 0) return notFoundResponse('Ticket', id); before data[0] access.

findings.description: sanitizeFilterValue does not strip period character. PostgREST uses periods as field.op.value delimiters. Input containing periods (e.g. 'v2.0') corrupts .or() filter syntax.
findings.suggestion: Add period to the regex: return value.replace(/[,.()]/g, '');

findings.description: search_products interpolates sanitized user input into PostgREST .or() filter. With periods intact, inputs like 'v2.0' or 'Pro.' produce malformed filter strings that return 400 errors from PostgREST.
findings.suggestion: Strip periods in sanitizeFilterValue, or switch to individual .ilike() calls chained with .or() operator instead of string interpolation.

findings.description: get_ticket uses 'if (error || !data)' which conflates DB errors (connection timeout, auth failure) with not-found. Returns misleading 'Ticket not found' for any error type.
findings.suggestion: Check error.code === PGRST_NOT_FOUND for not-found, return dbErrorResponse(error) for all other errors. Match the pattern used in get_customer.

findings.description: countWhere throws on error, which causes Promise.all to reject immediately, discarding results from all other successful parallel queries. One failed count kills the entire summary.
findings.suggestion: Have countWhere return a Result type instead of throwing, or use Promise.allSettled and include partial results.

findings.description: close_ticket has TOCTOU: reads status, checks if closed, then updates in separate query. Two concurrent calls both pass the check and both execute the update, overwriting closed_at timestamp.
findings.suggestion: Use conditional update: .update({...}).eq('id', id).neq('status', 'closed').select(), then check if data is empty (meaning already closed).

findings.description: list_customers returns all matching rows with no LIMIT. Also applies to list_tickets, list_products, search_products. With thousands of records, responses exceed practical size limits.
findings.suggestion: Add optional limit parameter (default 100) and offset for pagination.

findings.description: search_products uses ILIKE on three columns (name, category, description) without indexes. Full table scan on large datasets.
findings.suggestion: Add GIN indexes on searchable text columns, or use Supabase full-text search.

findings.description: formatTicketListItem uses || instead of ?? for customer name fallback. Empty string customer name gets replaced with 'Unknown Customer' instead of showing the empty string.
Schema resource is a hardcoded static definition. If the actual DB schema diverges (migration without code update), Claude receives incorrect schema info and generates wrong queries.
findings.suggestion: Add a comment documenting this is intentionally static for the demo. For production, query information_schema.

findings.description: get_summary fires 12 separate count queries to Supabase. On cold-start free-tier projects, all 12 hit simultaneously, risking rate limits and connection pool exhaustion.
findings.suggestion: Consider using a single Supabase RPC (PostgreSQL function) that returns all counts in one query.

findings.description: create_ticket validates customer existence then inserts ticket. If customer is deleted between validation and insert, FK violation produces a generic DB error instead of a helpful message.
findings.suggestion: Catch FK violation error code (23503) and return a specific 'customer was deleted' message.

planAttacks.attack: No authentication layer - any connected client has full read/write access via service role key.
planAttacks.defense: MCP servers run locally via stdio. No network exposure. Service role is necessary for full DB access.

planAttacks.attack: No rate limiting - a buggy AI agent could spam write operations.
planAttacks.defense: MCP stdio transport is serialized. Claude Desktop won't spam requests.

planAttacks.attack: Single-table seed script with no migration tooling - schema changes require manual SQL.
planAttacks.defense: Fixed demo with known schema. Migrations add complexity for zero benefit here.

planAttacks.attack: Service role key gives full access. Compromised Claude Desktop could exfiltrate or destroy all data.
planAttacks.defense: True for production. Would need RLS policies and scoped tokens.
